response.justification: While Sarah demonstrates strong software engineering discipline and valuable geospatial domain expertise (rare skills), she lacks the primary technical requirement of modern C++ development experience. Her C++ work spans only 6 months at National Instruments over a decade ago. For a mid-senior individual contributor role operating critical routing pipelines, the absence of recent C++ or Rust experience presents unacceptable ramp-up risk. Her software craftsmanship and willingness to learn new stacks are positive signals but insufficient to offset the core technical gap.
strengths: Founder of GISCollective and contributor to Green Map—deep, production geospatial/mapping platform expertise that is rare and directly applicable to routing data domains
Demonstrated software craftsmanship: TDD advocate, created testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts), built testable systems across roles—signals quality discipline needed for reliable routing pipelines
Proven agility across full technical stacks: staff-level work spanning Node.js, TypeScript, DLang, Java, infrastructure—shows willingness to master new domains beyond current expertise
concerns: Minimal and stale C++ experience: only 6 months at National Instruments (Dec 2014–June 2015), over 10 years ago; no recent C++ commercial or open-source work—primary job requirement unmet
Zero Rust experience mentioned: no alternative language coverage when C++ is the primary required skill and Rust is explicitly listed as an acceptable substitute

No demonstrated production navigation or routing system experience: geospatial data platforms (GISCollective, Green Map) are mapping/collection tools, not real-time routing engines with fault tolerance and consistency requirements
